caption:
NYC Neighborhoods You Probably Didn’t Know Existed 📍Little Britain #nyc #NewYork #travel #food #visitnyc 

*location and more details are pinned in the comments*

transcription.text: Neighborhoods in New York City you probably didn't know exist, part one. This is Little Britain, located on Greenwich Avenue, and it's this quaint neighborhood that you wouldn't even know was here from the outside. But when you take a closer look, you'll see a spot called Tea and Sympathy where you can enjoy a proper English afternoon tea with cucumber sandwiches, Victoria cake, and some classic Earl Grey tea. And we're not done yet, because you can also visit the store beside it with loads of British treats like McVitie's Digestives and Cadbury chocolate. Oh, and say you're craving some authentic fish and chips, well just head over to Assault and Battery for some scrummy fried haddock. Or maybe you're in the mood for Shepherd's Pie, a scone, or Walker's Crisps, this authentic British grocery store, Meyers of Keswick, has you covered. And finally, you can pass by John Lennon's old apartment as you head to enjoy a pint of beer at the second oldest bar in New York City, The White Horse, a place where Dylan Thomas used to frequently visit. Follow for more New York City tours.
